controls.title: Sunset exact Feather, Sunset, and Sunrise cardinality
controls.objective: Every completed full Atlas Sunset creates exactly one new sealed Feather, exactly one immutable Sunset bound to it, and exactly one Sunrise bound to the same pair.
controls.applies_when: The semantic objective is a full Atlas Sunset or a closeout that claims Sunset completion.
required_evidence: Exact candidate inventory proves one Feather, one Sunset, and one Sunrise.
Schema and relationship validation prove the same Feather/Sunset pair across all three records.

controls.title: Lessons never self-promote
controls.objective: Observations and Golden Wing candidates gain no authority until human-controlled review absorbs the lesson into a real enforced control.
controls.applies_when: A Preview, Build, review, Sunset, or continuity action identifies, evaluates, records, or reuses a lesson.
required_evidence: The lesson remains observation evidence or a Golden Wing candidate before reviewed absorption.
Any claimed absorption names an ACTIVE assurance control and its enforcing source and test.

controls.title: Repository process preserves one transaction and isolated fallback
controls.objective: Prime repository work uses one exact transaction identity and one complete candidate while preserving Thread Engine as the normal publisher and Sword/Oathbringer as an independently executable alternate publisher.
controls.applies_when: The objective creates, repairs, reviews, readies, merges, reverts, or recovers a Prime repository candidate or changes repository-process doctrine or tooling.
required_evidence: The exact candidate binds transaction, base, route, paths, tree, validation, review, permanence mode, and rollback identity.
Any route takeover reconciles the existing branch, PR, head, and receipts before mutation and does not create duplicate work.
Normal and alternate publishers remain failure-isolated; shared schemas or fixtures do not create one common mutation dependency.

controls.title: Shardblade is one-use exact-head compare-and-swap permanence
controls.objective: Machine permanence occurs only from a current explicit Jayson Shardblade instruction for one unchanged exact candidate after required status, resolved review, Strikeforce GREEN, replay reservation, rollback proof, atomic expected-head binding, and canonical readback.
controls.applies_when: A Prime repository transaction proposes, authorizes, performs, reports, recovers, or changes Shardblade or any automated merge behavior.
required_evidence: The current instruction unambiguously grants transaction-scoped Shardblade authority and no standing approval is inferred.
The merge request binds the expected head SHA and rejects any head, tree, path, status, review, authority, or replay drift.
The receipt and canonical-main readback prove the exact result; ambiguous results use readback-only reconciliation and never blind retry.

controls.title: Every full Sunset requires exact Preview approval
controls.objective: No full Atlas Sunset candidate may be constructed, published, or declared complete without one user-visible Preview and explicit Jayson approval bound to the same semantic payload.
controls.applies_when: The semantic objective is a full Atlas Sunset, a Sunset repair, or any action that constructs, publishes, resumes, or claims completion of Sunset lifecycle records.
required_evidence: The exact Preview ID, Preview digest, semantic digest, scope, record plan, and permitted approval modes are present.
The explicit Jayson approval and generated request bind the unchanged Preview, semantic payload, and selected permanence mode.
Any changed scope, lesson harvest, source lock, route, or record plan invalidates approval before candidate output.

controls.title: Sunset save state survives route failure
controls.objective: A connector, executor, network, repository, or local-checkout failure may delay canonical Sunset publication but may not destroy, replace, duplicate, or falsely complete the approved save point.
controls.applies_when: A full Sunset is approved, blocked, resumed, rerouted, published, or recovered before canonical readback completes.
required_evidence: One route-neutral approved carrier binds the exact Preview, approval, semantic payload, record plan, selected route, and fallback routes.
A blocked route preserves the same carrier and transaction identity and creates no duplicate branch, PR, or Sunset.
Only canonical merged-main readback permits the terminal SUNSET COMPLETE claim.

controls.title: Sunset lessons receive explicit disposition and follow-up
controls.objective: Every full Atlas Sunset performs Lesson Harvest and truthfully records whether each observation remains local, reinforces an existing safeguard, requires Golden Wing candidacy, requires assurance absorption, or is rejected.
controls.applies_when: A Sunset Preview, candidate, review, closeout, Sunrise, Golden Wing, or assurance-control action evaluates or reuses a lesson.
required_evidence: Every observation has one permitted disposition and an evidence-backed rationale, or the Harvest has one exact no-lesson reason.
Existing Golden Wing references resolve and are carried into Sunrise; candidate and absorption dispositions remain explicit follow-up obligations.
No observation, Feather, Golden Wing candidate, CI result, or Strikeforce verdict self-promotes into doctrine or authority.
